reports.summary: Volatile but net-positive Indian session with Nifty and Sensex closing slightly higher after deep intraday cuts, IT and defence showing leadership, crude, dollar and VIX elevated, FIIs selling heavily while DIIs buy, and key regulatory shifts in mutual funds and trading rules shaping the medium-term landscape.

reports.title: Market Pulse Daily

reports.stance: Use the Holi holiday pause to reassess risk, tighten weak positions, and prepare staggered deployment plans rather than rushing in or out.
reports.mainDriver: The 2026 Iran war and effective disruption of traffic through the Strait of Hormuz have pushed crude sharply higher, triggered global equity risk-off and a sharp selloff in Indian equities on 2 March, even though local markets are shut on 3 March for Holi.
reports.summary: 3 March 2026 – Indian markets shut for Holi after a sharp risk-off selloff on 2 March triggered by the Iran war and oil spike; regime shifts to a crude- and FX-driven correction with defence and select infra as relative winners, FIIs selling, DIIs absorbing and policy walking a tightrope between capex and shock management.

reports.title: Market Pulse Daily
reports.subtitle: Risk-off correction
reports.stance: Defensive to selectively bullish – protect capital first, take only high-quality, low-leverage risk where relative strength is clear.
reports.mainDriver: US–Israel–Iran war, closure of the Strait of Hormuz and a sharp spike in crude, volatility and the US dollar are driving a global risk-off move that hit Indian equities hard today.
reports.summary: 2 March 2026 – Indian equities sold off sharply as the US–Israel–Iran war closed the Strait of Hormuz and sent crude, the dollar and volatility higher, triggering a risk-off correction led by autos, financials and aviation while defence, metals and select defensives held up; DIIs absorbed heavy FII selling, and the regime shifted toward cautious, quality-focused positioning.

reports.title: Market Pulse Daily
reports.subtitle: Weekend Edition (data as of 2026-03-18)
reports.stance: Aggressive Capital Preservation and Defensive Posturing
reports.mainDriver: The Indian equity market is facing an unprecedented tri-factor shock this morning. First, Brent crude oil has violently surged past $112 per barrel following Iranian military strikes on the South Pars gas field and retaliatory missile launches toward Qatar and Saudi Arabia, threatening the critical Strait of Hormuz chokepoint. Second, the US Federal Reserve held interest rates at 3.50%-3.75% while adjusting its 'dot plot' to project only a single rate cut in 2026, officially embedding a 'higher for longer' cost of capital regime driven by sticky 2.7% PCE inflation. Third, and most damaging for domestic indices, HDFC Bank's part-time Chairman Atanu Chakraborty has abruptly resigned citing 'ethical concerns' and practices not in congruence with his personal values. This toxic combination of an exogenous energy shock, tight global liquidity, and a severe governance crisis at India's highest-weighted stock will trigger indiscriminate algorithmic and institutional liquidation at the opening bell.
reports.summary: March 19, 2026: A massive regime shift. GIFT Nifty indicates a 500+ point crash driven by Brent crude spiking to $112 after Middle East infrastructure attacks, a hawkish US Fed holding rates and projecting only one cut, and a severe governance shock at HDFC Bank. The report advises extreme defensive posturing, avoiding cyclicals, and hiding in IT and Coal India.

reports.summary: March 16, 2026: The Indian equity market staged a deceptive 900-point relief rally on the Sensex, driven by short-covering as Brent crude temporarily cooled following a historic IEA reserve release. However, internal market breadth collapsed, FIIs dumped ₹10,716 Cr, and the VIX surged above 22, signaling a highly toxic, risk-off environment. The IDBI Bank divestment collapsed, shattering the PSU privatization narrative. Strategic focus must shift exclusively to domestic power infrastructure, telecom hardware, and capital preservation.
